MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Continued To Aug-02-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-10-2013 Continued To Jan-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Continued To Aug-09-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven D. Werth, Esq. of Low Ball & Lynch, Tel. 415.981.6630, for defendant and moving party; Christopher B. Dolan, Esq. and Joshua H. Watson, Esq. of Dolaw Law Firm, Tel. 415.421.2800, fro plaintiff and opposing party; Edgar W. Hawkyard, Esq. of the Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh, Tel. 925.825.9500 x*821 via Court Call.

RULING: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARIN EXPERIMENTAL TEACHING TRAINING AND ADVISING's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden but Plaintiff has raised a triable issue regarding whether Defendant Shannon Wills was acting within the course and scope of her employment at the time the accident occurred. See Watson Declaration in Opposition to Motion, Ex.3 ("Wills Declaration"). Wills was the most authoritative staff person at METTA. (Wills Declaration 9.) METTA had a "METTA Mentors" program. (Id.10.) Wills' job duties included helping the mentees in community building, helping the mentees in broadening their base of support and helping them in explorations of meditation. (Id.12-14.) On the night of the collision, Wills was with mentees who were participating in the program. Wills and the "METTA mentees" had been together at a meditation gathering in Santa Clara hosted by Mr. Mehta. Wills thought Mr. Mehta's work was compatible with and relevant to nonviolence work, and thought it was appropriate for mentees to be exposed to his work and ideas. (Id.21.) Wills and the mentees then viewed the City lights together as part of that activity, to reflect on it, and to strengthen bonds. (Id.) Wills stated that having the mentees with her in her vehicle at the time of the collision was an understandable exercise of her discretion and duties to engage in community building, broaden mentees' exposures to the nonviolence community, and provide mentees with exposure to the real world of non-violence. (Id.24.) D'Amico and its progeny do not apply here. "In a nutshell, the rule bars a party opposing summary judgment from filing a declaration that purports to impeach his or her own prior sworn testimony." Scalf v. D.B. Log Homes, Inc. (2005) 128 Cal. App. 4th 1510, 1522. Wills is not a party opposing summary judgment. In any event, her declaration is not directly contradictory of her responses to the requests for admission. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Susan Crozier, CSR # 4761
